title: Fast Pod cold-start: bake-and-cache (RunPod-style) + shrink image — decide image architecture BEFORE the cu130 rebuild
description:
RE-SCOPED 2026-07-04 (MPI-187 live findings inverted the original premise). This card now DECIDES the Pod image architecture, and it GATES MPI-189 (the cu130 rebuild) — settle the structure here first so we build the cu130 image the RIGHT way ONCE, not rebuild twice.

=== WHY RE-SCOPED (the original premise was wrong) ===
Original card (below, preserved) assumed 'image too big -> slow cold pull -> move ComfyUI OFF the image ONTO the /workspace volume'. But live on MPI-187 we watched RunPod's official image boot INSTANT + models download instant doing the OPPOSITE: it BAKES ComfyUI into the image (/opt/comfyui-baked) + cp -r to LOCAL overlay disk on boot (seconds, zero network), and RunPod FLEET-CACHES its own image across hosts (near-zero registry pull). Our slowness is NOT 'image too big' — it is (a) cold GHCR pull of a custom low-traffic image on every fresh host + (b) start.sh STREAMING ComfyUI/weights from the 1GB/s network volume. Moving ComfyUI TO the volume makes us stream MORE from the slow path — likely WORSE. So the winning direction is a HYBRID of what we found: bake-and-cache for boot speed + shrink the image so it pulls & fleet-caches faster. NOT volume-relocation (which also needs a risky aimdo .so relocation spike — why relocate the load-bearing allocator onto the slow disk?).

=== THE DECISION SPIKE (do this FIRST, cheap, decides everything) ===
The one unknown that forks the whole architecture: DOES RUNPOD FLEET-CACHE A CUSTOM LOW-TRAFFIC TEMPLATE IMAGE the way it caches its own first-party image? Test it: register the CURRENT product image as a RunPod TEMPLATE, deploy it several times on fresh/cold hosts (vary datacenter), measure image-pull time each cold deploy. 
  - If template-caching WARMS it (pull drops to seconds after first use) -> PATH B/HYBRID wins: bake everything into the cu130 image + register a template + shrink. Skip volume-relocation entirely. This is the target.
  - If it does NOT warm (custom images not fleet-pre-cached) -> the bake still gives fast BOOT (cp -r local, no volume stream) but the PULL stays cold -> shrink becomes the primary lever (smaller image = faster cold pull); also test Docker Hub vs GHCR pull speed, and whether a RunPod-hosted registry / their image cache helps.
Either branch, volume-relocation of ComfyUI is OFF the table (loses to bake-and-cache). All live Pod ops USER-only.

=== SCOPE (hybrid = bake-and-cache + shrink) ===
1. BAKE + LOCAL-COPY: keep ComfyUI + deps BAKED in the image (like RunPod's /opt/comfyui-baked); on boot cp -r to container/local disk, NOT stream from the volume. start.sh: stop volume-streaming ComfyUI core. Volume holds MODELS ONLY (arch-agnostic, already the case for weights).
2. FLEET-CACHE: register a RunPod template for the image so it pre-caches across hosts (pending the spike result).
3. SHRINK: cut image size where free — multi-stage build discipline, drop build-only toolchains from the runtime layer, prune apt caches / pip caches / .pyc, remove any dev/devel base cruft not needed at runtime, squash where safe. Measure before/after. (torch trio + sage stay baked — arch-bound; that's the big weight but it's the price of a self-contained fast-boot image.)
4. REGISTRY: evaluate Docker Hub vs GHCR pull speed from RunPod hosts; move if materially faster.

=== RELATIONSHIP TO MPI-189 (SEQUENCE: this BEFORE 189) ===
This card's DECISION (bake-and-cache confirmed, shrink targets identified, registry chosen) feeds directly into HOW MPI-189 builds the single cu130 image. Do NOT build the cu130 image (189) until this architecture is settled — otherwise 189 builds it the old way and we rebuild again. Order: MPI-186 (decide+prototype architecture on the CURRENT image) -> MPI-189 (build the cu130 image THAT way) -> MPI-188 (guard, alongside 189) -> MPI-190 (bf16). The shrink + bake work itself can land in the SAME cu130 rebuild (189) — this card produces the DESIGN + the spike evidence; 189 executes it on cu130.

=== KEEP FROM THE ORIGINAL INVESTIGATION ===
research/investigation.md's dep classification + the aimdo/kitchen relocation-risk analysis stay useful REFERENCE (they prove WHY volume-relocation is risky = another reason to prefer bake-and-cache). The version-signal wiring ($CUBRIC_COMFYUI_REF -> manifest) is still valid if any skip-on-warm logic is wanted for the LOCAL-copy step. BRANCH: mpi-ci (Dockerfile/start.sh) + RunPod branch of Cubric-Vision if app-side template/manifest wiring is added.

=== ORIGINAL CARD (pre-cu130 premise, PRESERVED) ===
The product Pod image (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod) bakes ComfyUI + its ~5GB uncompressed pip dep tree into a Docker layer. On a network volume that layer re-pulls on every cold host and can never be skipped. [Original GOAL was: move ComfyUI SOURCE + arch-agnostic pip deps ONTO the /workspace volume, install once per volume via a boot-time script gated on $CUBRIC_COMFYUI_REF, skip when unchanged. Torch trio + sage stay baked (arch-bound). ComfyUI core + deps ONLY; 7 node packs stay baked, declared in extra_model_paths.yaml. Spike-gated on aimdo/kitchen surviving pip --target relocation.] This direction is now SUPERSEDED by bake-and-cache above — the volume-relocation fights the slow path instead of avoiding it.
activeSessionTitle: MPI-186 decision: GHCR->Docker Hub + multi-stage shrink (feeds cu130 rebuild MPI-189)
